description: A large area of flat land with few trees. 'Wemo' names the wide open—grasslands and prairies where sky meets earth uninterrupted, where one can see to the horizon in all directions.
sound_symbolism: The word spreads wide and level. 'We' begins with open glide, the expanse unfolding. Then 'mo' grounds in level earth, the flat surface continuing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo wemo' (the plain). Takes the elemental classifier 'themo' when counted. Associated with openness, grassland, and unobstructed horizons.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Plains sustained great herds and the peoples who followed them—open lands of grass, wind, and wide sky.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Plains teach equanimity—the level ground treats all equally, the open sky offers the same vastness everywhere.
